title: Crimes Of The Hot

jokes.text: Hurry up with the water. I'm steaming inside my own shell, I am. It's that hot, it is.
jokes.setup: Opening with heat wave, character complaining about temperature
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's characteristically awkward phrasing and self-evident observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg verbal mannerisms and redundant observations - solid character comedy

jokes.text: Man, it's hot. How hot is it? It's so hot, I poured McDonald's coffee in my lap to cool off.
jokes.setup: Standard 'how hot is it' setup format
jokes.punchline: Absurd reversal - McDonald's coffee notorious for being too hot, now used as coolant
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of the McDonald's hot coffee lawsuit/meme - well-structured joke

jokes.text: Johnny Carson said it.
jokes.setup: After delivering the hot weather joke
jokes.punchline: Fry crediting Johnny Carson for what sounds like his own joke
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about comedy attribution, fits Fry's character but fairly minor

jokes.text: No, Nibbler! Don't drink the pool water! It's full of chlorine. [Nibbler groaning, burping, coughing]
jokes.setup: Warning about chlorinated pool water
jokes.punchline: Nibbler drinks it anyway and has immediate physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard pet misbehavior gag with predictable payoff

jokes.text: Lightweights. Oh, wait, chlorine.
jokes.setup: Bender mocking others' reaction to chlorine
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization he's also affected, undermining his superiority
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender hubris immediately deflated - good character work and timing

jokes.text: Global Warming or: None Like It Hot!
jokes.setup: Educational film title announcement
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Some Like It Hot' movie title
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pun on classic film title - functional but not inspired

jokes.text: You're probably wondering why your ice cream went away. Well, Suzie, the culprit isn't foreigners. It's global warming.
jokes.setup: Educational film addressing child about missing ice cream
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing xenophobia as wrong cause before revealing climate apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about scapegoating and explaining apocalypse to children - good satirical edge

jokes.text: Gwobal wap-po? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Child trying to repeat 'global warming'
jokes.punchline: Adorable mispronunciation acknowledged matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Simple cute kid mispronunciation - minor but endearing

jokes.text: Hello, Earth. Just popping in to brighten your day. La-la-la-la-la-la-la. And now I'll be on my way. Not so fast, sunbeam. We're greenhouse gases. You ain't going nowhere.
jokes.setup: Anthropomorphized sunbeam cheerfully visiting Earth
jokes.punchline: Menacing greenhouse gases trapping the innocent sunbeam like gangsters
jokes.explanation: Creative anthropomorphization with noir/gangster dialogue - imaginative absurdist concept

jokes.text: Oh, God, it hurts! Pretty soon, Earth is chock-full of sunbeams... their rotting corpses heating our atmosphere.
jokes.setup: Sunbeam being tortured by greenhouse gases
jokes.punchline: Educational film matter-of-factly describing 'rotting corpses' of sunbeams
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdism - taking the sunbeam metaphor to grotesque logical conclusion

jokes.text: Ever since 2063, we simply drop a giant ice cube... into the ocean every now and then. Just like Daddy puts in his drink every morning. And then he gets mad.
jokes.setup: Explaining global warming solution with ice cubes
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent observation about alcoholic father's morning drinking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child casually revealing family dysfunction while discussing climate solutions

jokes.text: Thus, solving the problem once and for all. But-- Once and for all!
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining temporary ice cube solution
jokes.punchline: Aggressively cutting off any questioning of the obviously flawed logic
jokes.explanation: Satirizes political/corporate unwillingness to address systemic problems - good authoritarian voice

jokes.text: President Nixon here. I'm hiring you losers to deliver the ice. And hurry up. I'm sweating like J. Edgar Hoover trying to squeeze into a new girdle.
jokes.setup: Nixon calling to hire Planet Express for ice delivery
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific simile about J. Edgar Hoover in women's undergarments
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama Nixon - historically informed absurdist imagery perfectly in character

jokes.text: You heard the good news, everyone. Save the Earth, et cetera, et cetera. Bye.
jokes.setup: Major mission to save the planet
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's dismissive, perfunctory delivery of world-saving mission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth - treating apocalyptic mission as routine bureaucracy

jokes.text: Wait. Where do we get the ice? The what? Oh, Halley's comet, of course. The only sufficient source of ice cubes that don't have bugs in them.
jokes.setup: Logical question about ice procurement
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's complete lack of planning, then absurd specificity about bug-free ice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Farnsworth's absent-minded genius and odd priorities

jokes.text: Wow. Mining a comet. That sounds fun. Yes. There's no safer occupation than mining. Especially when you're perched on a snowball... whipping through space at a million miles an hour. Whoo! Whoo! Whoo! Whoo! Safe.
jokes.setup: Fry's naive enthusiasm about comet mining
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's increasingly sarcastic enumeration of the obvious dangers
jokes.explanation: Good escalating sarcasm with sound effects - plays well to both characters

jokes.text: Wow. That ice dispenser's so big the ice crushes you. Yakov Smirnoff said it. No, he didn't.
jokes.setup: Fry making Soviet Russia reversal joke
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately calling out the misattribution
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier comedy attribution - Fry's tendency to misattribute jokes

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's out of ice! Like some outer-space Motel 6.
jokes.setup: Discovery that Halley's comet is depleted
jokes.punchline: Fry comparing cosmic disaster to cheap motel experience
jokes.explanation: Fry's knack for mundane comparisons to cosmic events - good character voice

jokes.text: Completely out of ice? This could mean the end of the banana daiquiri as we know it. Also life.
jokes.setup: Catastrophic realization about Earth's fate
jokes.punchline: Bender prioritizing cocktails over human survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - brilliant structure with 'Also life' as throwaway afterthought

jokes.text: Morbo is pleased but sticky.
jokes.setup: News anchor reporting on global warming effects
jokes.punchline: Alien conqueror character admitting minor personal discomfort
jokes.explanation: Classic Morbo - evil alien dealing with mundane physical discomfort

jokes.text: They called me crazy for building this ark. You are crazy. You filled it with same-sex animal couples.
jokes.setup: Biblical ark reference during floods
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the ark is filled with gay animals, making it useless for repopulation
jokes.explanation: Subversive biblical reference with social commentary - good absurdist twist

jokes.text: Hey! There are parts of the Bible I like and parts I don't like.
jokes.setup: Defense of his selective biblical interpretation
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of cherry-picking religious doctrine
jokes.explanation: Commentary on selective religious interpretation - straightforward but effective

jokes.text: I'm sure those windmills will keep them cool. Windmills do not work that way! Good night!
jokes.setup: Co-anchor's innocent comment about windmills cooling turtles
jokes.punchline: Morbo's explosive scientific correction and abrupt sign-off
jokes.explanation: Iconic Morbo moment - became widely quoted meme for correcting scientific misconceptions

jokes.text: Well, everybody, I just saved a turtle. What have you done with your lives?
jokes.setup: Bender claiming moral superiority
jokes.punchline: Condescending question based on minimal charitable action
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender ego and moral grandstanding - good character consistency

jokes.text: Because I also care deeply about things that remind me of myself. Like poor little Shelly here.
jokes.setup: Question about why Bender cares about turtle
jokes.punchline: Admits he only cares about things that remind him of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness disguised as compassion - good character insight

jokes.text: What could you possibly have in common with this walking soup mix?
jokes.setup: Questioning Bender's connection to turtle
jokes.punchline: Describing turtle as 'walking soup mix'
jokes.explanation: Leela's practical/cynical perspective - economical and vivid description

jokes.text: You're both alcoholic, whore-mongering, chain-smoking gamblers? No. It's just... neither of us can get up when we get knocked on our back.
jokes.setup: Fry listing Bender's many vices as potential commonalities
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals innocent physical limitation as the actual connection
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up expectation of moral failings, delivers innocent physical fact

jokes.text: What? I've seen you get up off your back tons of times. Those times I was slightly on my side.
jokes.setup: Leela challenging Bender's claim about getting up
jokes.punchline: Bender's absurdly specific technical distinction
jokes.explanation: Bender's pedantic defense of his limitation - good absurdist logic

jokes.text: Maybe we should test this erectile dysfunction of yours, Bender.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bender's inability to get up
jokes.punchline: Leela using medical terminology with sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That's what I get for sharing my vulnerability with you.
jokes.setup: After being mocked for his limitation
jokes.punchline: Bender's rare moment of genuine emotional complaint
jokes.explanation: Rare glimpse of Bender's actual feelings - character development moment

jokes.text: I've got a degree in homeopathic medicine. You've got a degree in baloney.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claiming scientific credentials
jokes.punchline: Fry dismissing homeopathy as 'baloney'
jokes.explanation: Standard skepticism of alternative medicine - good character voices

jokes.text: You haven't acted this suspicious since I found the 'ape bones' in the basement.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Farnsworth's evasiveness
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to mysterious 'ape bones' discovery
jokes.explanation: Implies dark Farnsworth experiments - good mysterious backstory hint

jokes.text: My hip hurts. I'm in the middle of cooking a turkey. I have warranty cards to fill out. I am not just making excuses.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth avoiding the climate conference
jokes.punchline: Increasingly mundane excuses while claiming not to make excuses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of trivial excuses with self-aware denial - classic Farnsworth

jokes.text: His wristwatch is a Casio.
jokes.setup: Describing Farnsworth's appearance
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific mundane detail in celebrity coverage
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity in celebrity reporting - economical absurdist detail

jokes.text: The inventor of the environment and first emperor of the moon, Al Gore.
jokes.setup: Introducing Al Gore at climate conference
jokes.punchline: Absurd credentials including 'inventor of the environment' and lunar royalty
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist inflation of Gore's environmental credentials - perfect Futurama logic

jokes.text: I have ridden the mighty moon worm.
jokes.setup: Al Gore making opening statement
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about fantastical lunar creature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist character moment - perfectly captures Futurama's Gore as space adventurer

jokes.text: As I discuss in my book, Earth in the Balance... and the much more popular Harry Potter and the Balance of Earth...
jokes.setup: Gore referencing his environmental book
jokes.punchline: Fictional Harry Potter crossover version that's 'much more popular'
jokes.explanation: Commentary on pop culture vs. serious topics - decent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Sure, blame the wizards.
jokes.setup: Gore blaming dark wizards for pollution
jokes.punchline: Defensive response from actual wizard in audience
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat playing off Gore's wizard comment - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: With those, I could open the gate of Girash.
jokes.setup: Looking at moon sapphires prize
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to mystical gate opening
jokes.explanation: Wizard character having mundane reaction to magical opportunity - good world-building

jokes.text: Wornstrum!
jokes.setup: Professor Wornstrum being introduced
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's dramatic, angry recognition
jokes.explanation: Sets up rivalry dynamic - functional character moment

jokes.text: That's a little bright.
jokes.setup: Giant mirror reflecting sun causes mass blindness
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the catastrophic result
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor - perfect timing after visual catastrophe

jokes.text: Absolutely not! I won't speak. I've got nothing to hide.
jokes.setup: Asked to present his ideas
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous refusal to speak and claim of innocence
jokes.explanation: Classic suspicious behavior - obvious guilt while claiming innocence

jokes.text: I demand the floor! Yes, it's your turn to speak. Well, nuts to me. I'm taking the stage.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's contradictory behavior about speaking
jokes.punchline: Arguing with himself and dismissing his own position
jokes.explanation: Farnsworth's internal contradiction played for comedy - good character writing

jokes.text: Don't use this memory ray so I can remember what happened back then?
jokes.setup: Robot warning him not to do something
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was about to use sci-fi device for exposition
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about exposition devices - clever but not exceptional

jokes.text: It was October 17 at 1:54 p.m. and 14 birds were flying by the window.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth using memory ray to recall important past event
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific details about irrelevant background elements
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overly detailed memory recall, shows scientific mind focuses on wrong details

jokes.text: Oh, dear, might I favor master with a tender kiss on the forehead? Oh, I've failed again. Oh, dear. Oh, dear. Oh, dear.
jokes.setup: Flashback to old-style robots
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, self-flagellating robot with repeated failures
jokes.explanation: Good contrast setup for modern robots - establishes Victorian robot personality

jokes.text: What was that? A flaming burp. Does it always do that? It's not always a burp.
jokes.setup: Robot emitting flames
jokes.punchline: Implication that robot sometimes emits flames from other orifices
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective escalation - implies worse emissions without being explicit

jokes.text: Crap spackle! We'll just call it a sport utility robot and classify it as a light truck.
jokes.setup: Environmental concerns about robot emissions
jokes.punchline: Real-world regulatory loophole applied to robots
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of automotive industry emissions dodge - very relevant social commentary

jokes.text: Well... I suppose the environment can take one more for the team.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth considering environmental impact
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of environmental destruction as team player sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good environmental callousness disguised as team spirit - fits character's moral flexibility

jokes.text: That's awful, professor. Especially the making-out part. Yeah, I didn't need to hear that.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's confession about past mistakes and romance
jokes.punchline: Crew more disturbed by elderly romance than environmental catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Good priority reversal - focuses on personal discomfort over global disaster

jokes.text: I got a good feeling about this.
jokes.setup: About to hear plan to deal with robots
jokes.punchline: Bender optimistic about plan that will likely target robots
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony - Bender unaware of impending robot targeting

jokes.text: We must immediately and permanently... shut down, dismantle and destroy all robots!
jokes.setup: Building to solution for global warming
jokes.punchline: Extreme final solution targeting all robots
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to genocidal solution - dark turn fitting the environmental crisis theme

jokes.text: I must go now to help collect cans on Jupiter. Peace out, y'all.
jokes.setup: Gore leaving after dramatic pronouncement
jokes.punchline: Casual exit to do environmental work on Jupiter
jokes.explanation: Good character exit with space-age environmentalism - fits Futurama Gore

jokes.text: Yes! You tried to trick me into coming out of my chest. But who has the beer now?
jokes.setup: Bender hiding in his chest compartment
jokes.punchline: Triumphant about stealing beer while being coaxed out
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender priorities - more concerned with beer theft than survival

jokes.text: Strange. Why would Nixon, an awkward, uncomfortable man, throw a party? One of the most social events imaginable?
jokes.setup: Nixon throwing a robot party invitation
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Nixon's anti-social personality makes party suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Nixon's personality - logical suspicion

jokes.text: I don't hear any gasping. We all figured that out.
jokes.setup: Bender proudly deducing it's a trap
jokes.punchline: Everyone already figured it out, deflating Bender's detective moment
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bender's ego - everyone's ahead of his deduction

jokes.text: We can hide you. We'll pile fruit on you and claim you're a bowl.
jokes.setup: Trying to help Bender hide from robot hunters
jokes.punchline: Absurd disguise plan that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Fry's characteristically nonsensical but well-meaning plan - good absurdist logic

jokes.text: Fry, as you know, there are lots of things I'm willing to kill for. Jewels, vengeance, Father O'Malley's weed whacker.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his motivations
jokes.punchline: List escalates from noble to petty with oddly specific lawn equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - mixing criminal ambition with petty theft, oddly specific reference

jokes.text: This mindless turtle.
jokes.setup: Explaining what he's willing to die for
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of the turtle he's supposedly protecting
jokes.explanation: Bender's contradictory nature - dying for something he calls mindless

jokes.text: Bender, this world isn't good enough for you. Not even close.
jokes.setup: Bender's sacrifice speech
jokes.punchline: Fry's sincere but backhanded compliment about Bender's superiority
jokes.explanation: Fry's loyalty mixed with acknowledgment of Bender's arrogance - good friendship moment

jokes.text: Don't touch my stuff after I'm dead. It's booby-trapped!
jokes.setup: Bender's final goodbye
jokes.punchline: Even in death, protecting his possessions with traps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - selfishness extending beyond death, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Crummy keg, it's completely tapped-- Oh, you're a robot. Don't stop.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about empty keg
jokes.punchline: Realizes robot can dispense beer, suggestive phrasing
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre with beer dispensing - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: I'm going out to pick up some smokes. Really good smokes. Two hours.
jokes.setup: Nixon leaving robot party
jokes.punchline: Transparent excuse while emphasizing timing before mass execution
jokes.explanation: Nixon's bad lying and emphasis on timing - good villainous transparency

jokes.text: Very well. Agnew, you belong to Wornstrum now.
jokes.setup: Nixon making deal with Wornstrum
jokes.punchline: Casually trading his companion like property
jokes.explanation: Nixon's casual betrayal - good character consistency with his ruthlessness

jokes.text: Don't tell anybody, but Nixon's about to shut all the robots off and-- What?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to warn other robots
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-warning by the very thing he's warning about
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - warning interrupted by the threat itself

jokes.text: There ain't no turtles where Daddy's going.
jokes.setup: Bender saying goodbye to his turtle
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to afterlife while calling himself 'Daddy'
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing parental affection with death acceptance

jokes.text: Nixon's not bringing the smokes.
jokes.setup: Robots realizing they're about to be destroyed
jokes.punchline: Focusing on Nixon's lie about cigarettes rather than impending doom
jokes.explanation: Good callback and priorities reversal - minor lie overshadows major threat

jokes.text: Except perhaps you, Hedonism Bot.
jokes.setup: Bender talking about loving life's pleasures
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging that Hedonism Bot exceeds even him in pleasure-seeking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender acknowledging someone who out-hedons him

jokes.text: I apologize for nothing.
jokes.setup: Being called out for excessive hedonism
jokes.punchline: Completely unapologetic response perfectly in character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hedonism Bot line - completely embodies the character, highly quotable

jokes.text: To hell with the turtles! No one insults the turtles!
jokes.setup: Robot dismissing turtle conservation
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate violent defense of turtle honor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to violence over turtle insult - fits Bender's new obsession

jokes.text: By my calculations, we'll need the full force of every last robot. So quickly, everyone point your exhaust vents straight upward.
jokes.setup: Scientific plan to save Earth using robot emissions
jokes.punchline: Asks all robots to point their butts up and fart in unison
jokes.explanation: Clever setup for mass robot flatulence as scientific solution

jokes.text: Some lazy or polite robot is holding it in.
jokes.setup: Plan not working to push Earth away from sun
jokes.punchline: Blames failure on robot with good manners or work ethic
jokes.explanation: Funny blame assignment that treats politeness and laziness as equivalent problems

jokes.text: At least we'll die on our backs, helpless.
jokes.setup: Bender fallen on his back during crisis
jokes.punchline: References earlier turtle/robot similarity about being helpless when flipped
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier turtle comparison - brings story full circle with irony

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? Hey, are you trying to make me look bad?
jokes.setup: Turtle manages to get up while Bender can't
jokes.punchline: Bender accusing turtle of showing him up
jokes.explanation: Great payoff - turtle proves more capable than robot, Bender's ego wounded by being outdone

jokes.text: Hey, professor? Now that the Earth's orbit... is further from the sun, won't that make the year longer? Why, yes. One week longer to be exact.
jokes.setup: Logical question about orbital mechanics
jokes.punchline: Absurdly precise but convenient answer
jokes.explanation: Good setup for final gag - convenient science for plot purposes

jokes.text: In that case, I hereby declare it Robot Party Week!
jokes.setup: Learning the year is now longer
jokes.punchline: Nixon declaring holiday for robots he just tried to destroy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending - Nixon celebrating the robots he tried to eliminate

tmdb_overview: When Earth is jeopardized by global warming, the crew attends a conference in Kyoto hosted by Al Gore's head.
